methods.name: fa_purity._transform.cmd.CmdTransform.serial_merge
documentation:
Create a new command that will execute the supplied commands
in sequential order when computed

methods.name: fa_purity.json._transform.value._transform.JsonUnfolder.optional
documentation:
Get some specific key on the `JsonObj`
- return empty if key is missing
- return empty if value is `None`
- else apply the supplied transform

classes.name: fa_purity._core.bool.Bool
documentation:
Alternative to primitive bool.
- useful to avoid `if-else` syntax
- lazy evaluation on map

classes.name: fa_purity._core.unsafe.Unsafe

methods.name: fa_purity._core.unsafe.Unsafe.pure_iter_from_cmd
documentation:
The supplied commnad argument MUST produce semanticly equivalent iterables.
This is: possibly different objects that represents the same thing

- if Iterable is IMMUTABLE (e.g. tuple) then requirement is fulfilled
- if Iterable is MUTABLE then the commnad must call the obj constructor (that is not pure)
with the same arguments for ensuring equivalence.

Non compliant code:
  y = map(lambda i: i + 1, range(0, 10))
  x = unsafe_create_pure_iter(
      Cmd.from_impure(lambda: y)
  )
  # y is a map obj instance; cmd lambda is pinned with a single ref
  # since map is MUTABLE the ref should change at every call

Compliant code:
  x = unsafe_create_pure_iter(
      Cmd.from_impure(
          lambda: map(lambda i: i + 1, range(0, 10))
      )
  )
  # cmd lambda produces a new ref in each call
  # but all of them are equivalent (created with the same args)

methods.name: fa_purity._core.unsafe.Unsafe.stream_from_cmd
documentation:
As with `unsafe_create_pure_iter` the cmd must return a new iterable
object in each call to ensure that the stream is never consumed,
nevertheless they can be semanticly different iterables.

methods.name: fa_purity._core.unsafe.Unsafe.compute
documentation:
[WARNING] this will execute the Cmd
[NOTICE] If you what to define a new `Cmd` using this,
first consider using the `Cmd.new_cmd` constructor instead.

Only use when:
- all executions of the action `Cmd[_A]` result in the same
output instance `_A`
- side effects are not present or negligible

e.g. unwrap a cmd when used on a cached function definition

methods.name: fa_purity._core.unsafe.Unsafe.raise_exception
documentation:
Function wrapper of the raise statement.
This is unsafe since it should be not possible to
raise an error on a pure program.
Consider returning a `Result` object instead.

This class holds unsafe methods.
- Type-check cannot ensure its proper use
- Do not use until is strictly necessary
- Do unit test over the function defined by these

methods.name: fa_purity._core.pure_iter._factory.PureIterFactory.infinite_gen
documentation:
Generate an infinite PureIter starting from init,
the next item is the result of applying the function over the previous element.

methods.name: fa_purity._core.pure_iter._factory.PureIterFactory.infinite_range
documentation:
Infinite PureIter starting from start (first-arg).
The next element is the previous one + the  supplied step (second-arg)

methods.name: fa_purity._core.pure_iter._factory.PureIterFactory.pure_map
documentation: As built-in map function but produces a PureIter instead of a normal iterable.

methods.name: fa_purity._core.stream._factory.StreamFactory.generate
documentation:
Generate a `Stream` from a command that depends on a previous state
that depends from the result of the previous stream command.

- `get` = the command that depends on a previous state
- `extract` = how to derive the state from the result of the previous command.
    This also determines the stream end when returning empty.
- `init` = initial state value

classes.name: fa_purity._core.unit.UnitType
documentation:
Alternative to `None`.
The main issue with `None` is that its type definition is not clear,
`NoneType` was introduced and removed from typings.
To patch it, `UnitType` is the type and `UnitType()` or `unit` is the instance.

methods.name: fa_purity._core.pure_iter._core.PureIter.generate
documentation:
Generate a new iterable by applying the function over each item where:
- The argument state (_S) comes from a previous computation of the function over
a previous state-item pair or from `init_state` if it is the first element.

methods.name: fa_purity._core.pure_iter._core.PureIter.enumerate
documentation:
Generate a new iterable by enumerating each item
starting from the suplied init integer.

methods.name: fa_purity._core.pure_iter._core.PureIter.map
documentation:
Generate a new iterable by applying the supplied function
to each of the element of the current iterable.

methods.name: fa_purity._core.pure_iter._core.PureIter.reduce
documentation:
Apply the supplied function to the iterable such that:
- the second argument `_T` is the current element in the iteration
- The first argument `_R` is the result of applying the function
over the previous element i.e. function(previous_item). If there is not
a previous element then the argument `_R` is set to the supplied init
- This returns the last result of the function call or init if
iterator is empty

classes.name: fa_purity._core.coproduct.Coproduct
documentation:
Also know as the union type, `Coproduct` unambiguously
differentiate between the left and right case. This enables
a better way of handling generic union types where `isinstance`
cannot be used to differentiate the left-right cases.

e.g. Union[None, None] = None # left and right injections are equal
Coproduct[None, None] = Bool # left and right injections are NOT equal

classes.name: fa_purity._core.coproduct.CoproductFactory
documentation:
When type induction fails to recognize the left or right type,
this factory can be used to explicitly indicate the types.

classes.name: fa_purity._core.coproduct.UnionFactory
documentation:
When type induction fails to recognize the left or right type
on the built in Union this factory can help to set the types
explicitly.

variables.name: fa_purity._core.frozen.FrozenList
documentation: type: ignore[misc]

modules.name: fa_purity._core.iter_factory
documentation: Iterable transforms
should always return a new instance because Iterables are mutable
result should be wrapped in a Cmd

classes.name: fa_purity._core.cmd.Cmd

methods.name: fa_purity._core.cmd.Cmd.map
documentation:
Outputs a new procedure that when computed will:
- execute the command
- apply the supplied function to the result

methods.name: fa_purity._core.cmd.Cmd.bind
documentation:
Outputs a new procedure that when computed will:
- execute the command
- apply the supplied function to the result
- execute the resulting command

methods.name: fa_purity._core.cmd.Cmd.compute
documentation:
Executes the command.
- this should be the last thing the program will do

class_variables.name: fa_purity._core.cmd.Cmd._value
documentation: not a pure function!

Equivalent to haskell IO type.
This type handles impure commands with out executing them.

classes.name: fa_purity._core.cmd.CmdUnwrapper

methods.name: fa_purity._core.cmd.CmdUnwrapper.act
documentation:
[WARNING] Not a pure function.

This method is more safe than `unsafe_unwrap` since it
wraps the result into another `Cmd`, but is possible to
use it incorrectly in places where a pure function is
expected e.g. `PureIter.map` and cause unexpected bugs.

Object that allows cmd execution.

Instances can not be created by the user by design.
Only through the `Cmd.new_cmd` the user can use an
instance of this type.

methods.name: fa_purity.lock.ThreadLock.execute_with_lock
documentation:
Create a commnad that will execute the supplied command
in the context of the lock. i.e. acquire the lock,
execute the command and release the lock

variables.name: fa_purity._core.result.ResultE
documentation: type: ignore[misc]

classes.name: fa_purity._core.result.ResultFactory
documentation:
Generic types cannot be passed as type arguments
on success and failure constructors.
This factory handles the generic type use case.
